The NHL said the fine was issued for violation of rules prohibiting formal team statements to the media during the 48-hour period following a disciplinary decision. The rule calls for an automatic $25,000 fine, and the Sharks were docked an additional $75,000 under an article in the league's constitution because of the "inappropriate nature of the comments." Torres was suspended for the rest of San Jose's second-round playoff series against Los Angeles on Thursday for an illegal check to the head of Kings forward Jarret Stoll during Game 1.

prompt_text: A normal ear transmits sounds as shown in FIG. 1 through the outer ear 101 to the tympanic membrane 102 which moves the bones of the middle ear 103 that vibrate the oval window and round window openings of the cochlea 104. The cochlea 104 is a long narrow duct wound spirally about its axis for approximately two and a half turns.
base_text: A normal ear transmits sounds as shown in FIG. 1 through the outer ear 101 to the tympanic membrane 102 which moves the bones of the middle ear 103 that vibrate the oval window and round window openings of the cochlea 104. The cochlea 104 is a long narrow duct wound spirally about its axis for approximately two and a half turns. The cochlea contains fluid-filled chambers, the scala vestibuli, scala media, and scala tympani, which are separated by membranes. Sound vibrations cause fluid movement within these chambers, stimulating hair cells located on the basilar membrane of the scala media.
final_text: A normal ear transmits sounds as shown in FIG. 1 through the outer ear 101 to the tympanic membrane 102 which moves the bones of the middle ear 103 that vibrate the oval window and round window openings of the cochlea 104. The cochlea 104 is a long narrow duct wound spirally about its axis for approximately two and a half turns. The cochlea contains fluid-filled chambers, the scala vestibuli, scala media, and scala tydpani, which are separated by membranes. Sound vibrations cause fluid movement within these chambers, stimulating hair cells located on the basilar membrane of the scala media.

label: ai
final_text: Introduction { # s1 } ============ * Trypanosoma brucei * is an extracellular human pathogen with an unparalleled capacity to evade host humoral immunity. The causative agent of African sleeping sickness in humans and nagana in cattle, * T. brucei * is transmitted into the bloodstream of its host by a tsetse vector And can grow to densities as high as 10^9^ cells per milliliter of blood. The parasitemia is cyclically diminished to nearly undetectable levels, to be followed by another wave of immense growth [ @ ppat.1002900-Barry1 ]. These rounds of parasitemia reflect the battle between the host immune system and the pathogen\ 's mechanisms of immune evasion.

Magical Words () is a Canadian dramatic short film, directed by Jean-Marc Vallée and released in 1998. base_text:
Magical Words

Magical Words () is a Canadian dramatic short film, directed by Jean-Marc Vallée and released in 1998. A thematic sequel to his 1995 short film Magical Flowers (Les Mots magiques), the film centres on a 30 year old man (Richard Robitaille) finally confronting his father (Robert Gravel) about his alcoholism. Shot in a stark, minimalist style, the film explores themes of familial estrangement, regret, and the struggle for redemption. Vallée's direction emphasizes quiet emotional intensity, with sparse dialogue and lingering close-ups that capture the weight of unspoken tensions.
final_text:
Magical Words

Magical Words () is a Canadian dramatic short film, directed by Jean-Marc Vallée and released in 1998. A thematic sequel to his 1995 short film Magical Flowers (Les Mots magiques), the film centres on a 30 year old man (Richard Robitaille) finally confronting his father (Robert Gravel) about his alcoholism. Shot in a stark, minimalist style, the film explores themes of familial estrangement, regret, and the struggle for redemption. Vallée's direction emphasizes quiet emotional intensity, with sparse dialogue and lingering close-ups that capture the weight of unspoken tensions.

prompt_text: Retinal degenerations are a heterogeneous group of eye diseases that result in the permanent loss of vision and affect millions of individuals worldwide. Although the molecular mechanisms underlying these conditions vary, they share a common endpoint: the irreversible death of the photoreceptor cells. No effective treatment is currently available to restore lost photoreceptors and visual function and most therapeutic interventions can at best only slow down the disease progression. Prior clinical studies in patients with retinal degeneration have involved the use of fetal retinal sheet transplants.
base_text: Retinal degenerations are a heterogeneous group of eye diseases that result in the permanent loss of vision and affect millions of individuals worldwide. Although the molecular mechanisms underlying these conditions vary, they share a common endpoint: the irreversible death of the photoreceptor cells. No effective treatment is currently available to restore lost photoreceptors and visual function and most therapeutic interventions can at best only slow down the disease progression. Prior clinical studies in patients with retinal degeneration have involved the use of fetal retinal sheet transplants. However, these approaches have been limited by several challenges, including immune rejection, poor integration of transplanted cells into the host retina, and the difficulty of obtaining suitable donor tissue.
final_text: Retinal degenerations are a heterogeneous group of eye diseases that result in the permanent loss of vision and afect millions of individuals worldwide. Although the molecular mechanisms underlying these conditions vary, they share a common endpoint: the irreversible death of the photoreceptor cells. No effective treatment is currently available to restore lost photoreceptors and visual function and most therapeutic interventions can at best only slow down the disease progression. Prior clinical studies in patients with retinal degeneration have involved the use of fetal retinal sheet transplants. However, these approaches have been limited by several challenges, including immune rejection, poor integration of transplanted cells into the host retina, and the difficulty of obtaining suitable donor tissue.

final_text: Introduction { # Sec1 } ============ Hundreds of miRNAs regulate the expression of about 60 % of human protein-coding genes at the post-transcriptional level^ [ @ CR1 ] -- [ @ CR5 ] ^. The mature miRNAs associate with Argonaute ( AGO ) proteins to form an miRNA-induced silencing complex ( miRISC ), which causes downregulation of target genes through a variety of mechanisms^ [ @ CR6 ] -- [ @ CR9 ] ^. In animals, binding sites, denoted as `` seed matches ``, are mostly localized within 3′ UTRs and usually contain six nucleotides, which are complementary to bases 2 -- 7 of the 5′ end of the miRNA^ [ @ CR1 ], [ @ CR3 ], [ @ CR6 ] ^.

prompt_text:
Introduction
============

The therapeutic applications of antibodies pioneered by von [@b1] at the end of the 19th century were revolutionized by the advent of monoclonal antibody technology, and subsequently, improved by genetic engineering approaches such as humanization ([@b13]). To date, over 20 recombinant monoclonal antibodies have been licensed as drugs against various cancers and chronic diseases. Furthermore, antibody-based therapeutics currently account for most recombinant proteins in clinical use, and over 130 human monoclonal antibodies entered clinical trials between 2001 and 2008 ([@b18]; [@b21]). Although the demand for industrial production of recombinant monoclonal antibodies is increasing, and the number of approved antibodies for therapeutic uses will increase during the next few years, one drawback of antibody medicine is the high cost of production.

Although the demand for industrial production of recombinant monoclonal antibodies is increasing, and the number of approved antibodies for therapeutic uses will increase during the next few years, one drawback of antibody medicine is the high cost of production.

To date, over 20 recombinant monoclonal antibodies have been licensed as drugs against various cancers and chronic diseases. Furthermore, antibody-based therapeutics currently account for most recombinant proteins in clinical use, and over 130 human monoclonal antibodies entered clinical trials between 2001 and 2008 ( [ @ b18 ]; [ @ b21 ] ). Although the demand for industrial production of recombinant monoclonal antibodies is increasing, and the number of approved antibodies for therapeutic uses will increase during the next few years, one drawback of antibody medicine is the costt of production.

label: ai
final_text: Introduction { # s1 } ============ Sepsis is a complex clinical condition that results from the inability to of the host to regulate inflammatory responses against infection. Sepsis causes severe adverse outcomes such as shock, organ dysfunction, and death [ @ pone.0077936-Warren1 ]. Despite more than 30 years of extensive study, the pathophysiology of sepsis in humans remains poorly understood, and hospitalization and mortality rates of patients with sepsis have increased substantially [ @ pone.0077936-Dombrovskiy1 ]. Sepsis accounts for 25 % of admissions to the intensive care units ( ICUs ), and it is associated with a mortality rate of approximately 50 % [ @ pone.0077936-Engel1 ]; therefore, there is an urgent need to understand the pathophysiological processes underlying sepsis and identify biomarkers for early diagnosis or therapeutic intervention for this disease.

label: ai
final_text: Introduction { # s1 } ============ The follicular lymphoma ( FL ) is the second more histotype of non-Hodgkin\ 's lymphomas in the Western world, and represents one of the entities where the minimal residual disease ( MRD ) has been more frequently studied. Indeed, the history of MRD in FL is now 15 years old, starting with the demonstration that rituximab ( R ) significantly reduced the molecular tumor burden when administered after the CHOP ( Cyclophosphamide, Doxorubicin, Vincristine, Prednisone ) regimen: in that setting, patients achieving MRD negativity showed a significantly longer event-free survival ( Rambaldi et al., [ @ B14 ] ). Then, many other works focused on the MRD in the transplanted patients: cases MRD-negative after autologous transplantation remained relapse-free and alive in a higher percentage than those persistently MRD-positive ( Galimberti et al., [ @ B3 ]; Melillo et al., [ @ B9 ] ).

label: ai
prompt_text: Spectroscopy by grating synthesis. Spectral information obtained by synthesis of two or more diffraction gratings is studied.
base_text: Spectroscopy by grating synthesis. Spectral information obtained by synthesis of two or more diffraction gratings is studied. This technique leverages the constructive and destructive interference patterns generated by the superposition of multiple gratings, enabling higher resolution and broader wavelength coverage compared to single-grating systems. The synthesized grating arrangement can mitigate aberrations, improve dispersion uniformity, and extend the operational range into extreme ultraviolet or X-ray regimes when combined with advanced materials. Applications include high-precision astronomical spectroscopy, laser wavelength stabilization, and analysis of complex molecular spectra in chemical sensing.
final_text: Spectroscopy by grating synthesis. Spectral information obtainen by synthesis of two or more diffraction gratings is studied. This technique leverages the constructive and destructive interference patterns generated by the superposition of multiple gratings, enabling higher resolution and broader wavelength coverage compared to single-grating systems. The synthesized grating arrangement can mitigate aberrations, improve dispersion uniformity, and extend the operational range into extreme ultraviolet or X-ray regimes when combined with advanced materials. Applications include high-precision astronomical spectroscopy, laser wavelength stabilization, and analysis of complex molecular spectra in chemical sensing.

label: ai
prompt_text: Soft polypropylene mesh, but not cadaveric dermis, significantly improves outcomes in midline hernia repairs using the components separation technique. The search continues for the "ideal" repair of the midline ventral hernia, and the components separation technique has a low, but still concerning, hernia recurrence rate. The authors hypothesize that adding prosthetic or bioprosthetic meshes to the midline closure during components separation would reduce recurrence rates with minimal added morbidity. Over a 3-year period, patients had a components separation procedure where either acellular cadaveric dermis (n = 26) or soft polypropylene mesh (n = 28) was used as an intraperitoneal underlay for reinforcement of the midline repair, but not as a "bridging material."

label: ai
prompt_text:
Q:

Encoding a signed number using a custom alphabet

When encoding a sequence of bits into a string (using an alphabet that is not yet known at compile time and with the goal that the resulting string is as short as possible and that this whole process is reversible), you can use the "divmod approach", see e.g. this post. From my understanding, this appraoch only works for positive numbers. In my case, I want to encode 64 bit signed integers (Java's Long), so the numbers can be both positive and negative.
base_text:
Q:

Encoding a signed number using a custom alphabet

When encoding a sequence of bits into a string (using an alphabet that is not yet known at compile time and with the goal that the resulting string is as short as possible and that this whole process is reversible), you can use the "divmod approach", see e.g. this post. From my understanding, this appraoch only works for positive numbers. In my case, I want to encode 64 bit signed integers (Java's Long), so the numbers can be both positive and negative.
final_text: Q: Encoding a signed number using a custom alphabet When encoding a sequence of bits into a string ( using an alphabet that is not yet known at time and with the goal that the resulting string is as short as possible and that this whole process is reversible ), you can use the `` divmod approach '', see e.g. this post. From my understanding, this appraoch only works for positive numbers. In my case, I want to encode 64 bit signed integers ( Java 's Long ), so the numbers can be both positive and negative.

prompt_text: Image Informatics Strategies for Deciphering Neuronal Network Connectivity. Brain function relies on an intricate network of highly dynamic neuronal connections that rewires dramatically under the impulse of various external cues and pathological conditions. Amongst the neuronal structures that show morphological plasticity are neurites, synapses, dendritic spines and even nuclei. This structural remodelling is directly connected with functional changes such as intercellular communication and the associated calcium bursting behaviour.
base_text: Image Informatics Strategies for Deciphering Neuronal Network Connectivity. Brain function relies on an intricate network of highly dynamic neuronal connections that rewires dramatically under the impulse of various external cues and pathological conditions. Amongst the neuronal structures that show morphological plasticity are neurites, synapses, dendritic spines and even nuclei. This structural remodelling is directly connected with functional changes such as intercellular communication and the associated calcium bursting behaviour. Advanced imaging modalities, such as two-photon microscopy and electron tomography, coupled with sophisticated computational algorithms, enable the high-resolution mapping of these dynamic structures.
final_text: Image Informatics Strategies for Deciphering Neuronal Network Connectivity. Brain function relies on an intricate network of highly dynamic neuronal connections that rewires dramatically under the impulse of various external cues and pathological conditions. Amongst the neuronal structures that showw morphological plasticity are neurites, synapses, dendritic spines and even nuclei. This structural remodelling is directly connected with functional changes such as intercellular communication and the associated calcium bursting behaviour. Advanced imaging modalities, such as two-photon microscopy and electron tomography, coupled with sophisticated computational algorithms, enable the high-resolution mapping of these dynamic structures.

label: ai
final_text: Introduction { # s1 } ============ Mild traumatic brain injury ( mTBI ) has an under reported incidence of 100 -- 300 out of 100,000 cases and often gets little attention from the treating clinicians as it requires no emergent surgical treatment and has spontaneous recovery with predictable neurobehavioral sequelae ( Cassidy et al., [ @ B6 ] ). Around 5 % of patients ( Iverson, [ @ B20 ] ) develop `` post-concussion syndrome '' ( PCS ) characterized by physical,, cognitive, and sleep disturbances and may take many months to return to the baseline. Nevertheless, mTBI is considered a `` silent epidemic, '' given its incidence in economically active young min and so there is a priority in understanding the course of recovery and predictors for recovery in mTBI ( Cassidy et al., [ @ B6 ] ).

abstract: 'Numeric static analysis for Java has a broad range of potentially useful applications, including array bounds checking and resource usage estimation. However, designing a scalable numeric static analysis for real-world Java programs presents a multitude of design choices, each of which may interact with others. For example, an analysis could handle method calls via either a top-down or bottom-up interprocedural analysis.
base_text:
---
abstract: 'Numeric static analysis for Java has a broad range of potentially useful applications, including array bounds checking and resource usage estimation. However, designing a scalable numeric static analysis for real-world Java programs presents a multitude of design choices, each of which may interact with others. For example, an analysis could handle method calls via either a top-down or bottom-up interprocedural analysis. These choices are further complicated by the need to balance precision with performance, as overly conservative approximations can lead to false positives, while insufficient precision may result in missed errors. Additionally, the analysis must account for Java's complex type system, including generics, exceptions, and dynamic class loading, which introduce further challenges in modeling program behavior.
final_text: -- - abstract: 'Numeric static analysis for Java has a broad range of potentially useful applications, including array bounds checking and resource usage estimation. However, designing a scalable static analysis or real-world Java programs presents a multitude of design choices, each of which may interact with others. For example, an analysis could handle method calls via either a top-down or bottom-up interprocedural analysis. These choices are further complicated by the need to balance precision with performance, as overly conservative approximations can lead to false positives, while insufficient precision may result in missed errors. Additionally, the analysis must account for Java 's complex type system, including generics, exceptions, and dynamic class loading, which introduce further challenges in modeling program behavior.

prompt_text:
On becoming the new face of Chanel’s Mademoiselle handbag: “It’s such an honor. When I found out I got [the job], you and I went to dinner and concocted this plan, like two little girls at a tea party, saying, “Oh, it would be the best if we had a party. And you’ll perform, and we’ll do it in Paris.” Like you and I were in charge of Chanel!”

On being her own stylist: “I think I became my own stylist by not knowing any better.

prompt_text:
Czech Prime Minister: ‘We do not want to live in Africa!’

The head of the Czech government Andrej Babis takes illegal migration as a threat to the European civilization and approves anti-immigration protests from Chemnitz. He has taken a strong stance on the European Union’s migration policy. ‘Chemnitz is there, at the corner of the street!,’ he exclaimed on September 2 on a Czech television, quoted by the media Parlamentni Listy. ‘I have explained at length that this is a fight to preserve our European civilization and our culture.

DAMTP-2016-26

**Introduction**
================

In a previous paper [@ITD2] we studied a scalar field theory model in which each field was associated with a distinct metric. For simplicity we assumed that spacetime was represented by a flat background and parametrised by coordinates $x^\mu$ such that the theory is invariant under the translations $x^\mu\rightarrow x^\mu+a^\mu$.

label: ai
final_text: INTRODUCTION { # s1 } ============ Breast cancer is the second most common cancer worldwide with 1.7 million cases and over 522,000 deaths per year \ [ [ @ R1 ] \ ]. During the last years, numerous investigations have reported a complex signaling network between breast cancer cells and the tumor microenvironment which affects tumor malignance and therapeutic responsiveness \ [ [ @ R2 ] \ ]. Adipose tissue, a metabolically active endocrine organ, is the most abundant stromal constituent in the mammary gland surrounding breast cancer. This tissue shows a high secretive activity and is an extensive source of stem cells ( MSCs ) or more specifically adipose-derived stem/stroma cells ( ASCs ).

label: ai
final_text: Q: Got completely confused on how to OpenCL data transfer I 'm learning OpenCL and attempt to utilize it on some low-latency scenario, so I 'm really concerned with the memory transferring delay. According to NVidia 's OpenCL Best Practices Guide, and also by many other places, direct read/write on buffer object should be avoided. Instead, we should use map/unmap utility. In that guide, a demonstrative code is given like this: cl_mem cmPinnedBufIn = clCreateBuffer ( cxGPUContext, CL_MEM_READ_ONLY | CL_MEM_ALLOC_HOST_PTR, memSize, NULL, NULL ); cl_mem cmDevBufIn = clCreateBuffer ( cxGPUContext, CL_MEM_READ_ONLY, memSize, NULL, NULL ); unsigned char * cDataIn = ( cear * ) clEnqueueMapBuffer ( cqCommandQue, cmPinnedBufIn, CL_TRUE, CL_MAP_WRITE, 0, memSize, 0, NULL, NULL, NULL ); for ( unsigned int i = 0; i < memSize; i++ ) { cDataIn [ i ] = ( unsigned char ) ( i & 0xff ); } clEnqueueWriteBuffer ( cqCommandQue, cmDevBufIn, CL_FALSE, 0, szBuffBytes, cDataIn, 0, NULL, NULL ); In this code snippet, two buffer objects are generated explicitly, and a write-to-device operation is also explicitly called.

label: ai
final_text: Introduction { # s1 } ============ Iron deficiency anemia ( IDA ) is one of the commonest nutritional deficiencies globally, with young children, women of childbearing age and pregnant women at highest risk [ @ pone.0004030-Centres1 ]. Adequate iron stores are important for normal childhood growth and development [ @ pone.0004030-Centres1 ] and IDA may impair cognitive development [ @ pone.0004030-Lozoff1 ], [ @ pone.0004030-McCall1 ]. Nutritional deficiencies are particularly in resettled pediatric refugees, who often have significant co-morbidities ( including acute chronic infection and/or hemoglobinopathies ) which may exacerbate IDA [ @ pone.0004030-McGillvray1 ]. Hepcidin, a type II acute phase peptide, plays a central role in the regulation of iron homeostasis [ @ pone.0004030-Ganz1 ], [ @ pone.0004030-Kemna1 ].
